explanation: Mechanical removal of outer husk or hull from grain or seed.
Generic milling — use only when milling grade is unknown or not relevant. Prefer the specific variant below where known.
Milling that retains all bran and germ fractions. Produces whole grain flour (atta).
notes: Split from generic milling because whole vs refined produces legally distinct products with different labelling obligations.

explanation: Milling with bran and germ removed. Produces refined flour (maida).
Milling to coarse particle size retaining endosperm structure. Produces semolina / sooji / rava.
Controlled steeping followed by germination then kiln-drying of grain. Activates amylase enzymes.
notes: Added; not in EMF E-axis. Key process for malted wheat and malted barley forms.

explanation: Steaming of grain followed by rolling into flat flakes under pressure.
notes: Added; not in EMF. Produces oat flakes

explanation: Mechanical extraction of oil without applied heat. Preserves flavour and micronutrient profile.
Application of dry heat to grain
notes: nut
column5: or bean. Alters flavour and reduces moisture.

explanation: Mechanical agitation of cream to aggregate fat globules and separate buttermilk. Produces butter.
Controlled heat treatment below 100°C to reduce pathogen load without full sterilisation.
notes: Spelling standardised to British English for IFID consistency.

explanation: Heat treatment sufficient to eliminate all viable microbial activity. Covers UHT processing.
notes: Added; not in EMF. Required for UHT milk and shelf-stable dairy forms.

explanation: Mechanical disruption of fat globules to produce uniform stable dispersion in liquid.
notes: Added; not in EMF. Standard dairy step for liquid milk and cream products.

explanation: Separation of fat layer from liquid by centrifuge or gravity. Produces cream and skimmed liquid fractions.
notes: Added; not in EMF. Needed for skimmed milk

explanation: Water removal from liquid by controlled heat application. Produces concentrate or condensed forms.
notes: Added; not in EMF. Distinct from spray drying — product remains liquid or semi-liquid.

explanation: Atomisation of liquid feed into a hot air chamber to produce fine powder.
notes: Added; not in EMF. Standard method for milk powder

explanation: Water removal by sublimation under vacuum at low temperature. Preserves structure and bioactives.
notes: Added; not in EMF. Used for some extracts and premium ingredients.

explanation: Removal of suspended impurities from liquid by heating and straining. Key step in ghee production.
Microbial transformation of substrate by bacteria
notes: or fungi. Covers lactic acid fermentation and others.
column6: Use this value for all fermentation types. Specific culture or organism is not captured here.

explanation: Protein aggregation induced by acid
notes: heat
column10: Added; not in EMF. Distinct from fermentation — coagulation is physical/chemical protein change

explanation: Mechanical compression of coagulated mass to expel whey and form a cohesive block.
notes: Added; not in EMF. Distinguishes paneer (pressed) from chhena (unpressed) and hard cheese from soft.

explanation: Controlled maturation of cheese or other fermented form over time. Microbial and enzymatic activity continues post-production.
notes: Added; not in EMF. Required to distinguish aged cheese subtypes from fresh cheese.

explanation: Removal of fat fraction from solid or semi-solid material. Produces defatted meal or defatted flour.
notes: Added; not in EMF. Covers solvent and mechanical defatting for seed meals.

explanation: Chemical neutralisation and deodorisation of crude oil to remove free fatty acids and off-flavours.
Separation of a mixture into distinct fractions by controlled cooling or membrane separation.
notes: Covers both fat fractionation (stearin/olein) and milk fraction separation.

explanation: Membrane-based separation retaining large molecules (proteins) and passing small molecules (lactose
column5: Added; not in EMF. Required for WPC

explanation: Precipitation of protein by adjusting pH to the protein's isoelectric point. Used for casein isolation.
notes: Added; not in EMF.

explanation: Use of organic solvent to extract oil or specific compound from solid material.
Treatment with peroxide or chlorine compounds to whiten flour or oil.
notes: Added; not in EMF as a standalone step. Used for treated flour products.

explanation: Controlled cooling or concentration to produce crystalline solid form from solution or melt.
notes: Added; not in EMF as standalone. Covers sugar crystallisation

explanation: Enzymatic or chemical rearrangement of fatty acid positions on triglyceride molecule.
Addition of hydrogen across double bonds in unsaturated fatty acids under catalyst.
Chemical modification by reaction with acetic anhydride. Used for modified starches.
Forcing material through a die under heat and mechanical pressure. Produces pasta
column5: Added; not in EMF. Relevant when extruded forms appear as declared ingredients.
